escalatedRisks.risk: Samsung union vote — DX division revolt 🆕⚡
escalatedRisks.status: NEW ACTIVE — Day 1. NSEU 69.15% turnout Day 1. DX division legal+membership revolt.
escalatedRisks.source: Seoul Economic Daily / Korea Times / Chosun Ilbo / multiple May 22-23 2026
escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung union vote started 5/22, turnout 69.15% NSEU Day 1. DS chip employees ~5.09亿 won (~$335K) under new deal vs DX (mobile/TV/appliances) employees ~5000万 won (~$34K) at best, as low as 600万 won (~$4K). 100:1 bonus disparity. DX union (Donghaeng) grew 10,000-12,000 members in short period. DX employees campaigning to REJECT the deal. Court injunction filed to block DS-dominated union from handling collective bargaining. Korea Shareholder Action Headquarters threatens lawsuit — bonus deal bypassing shareholder approval. Key clause: both sides withdraw existing civil and criminal lawsuits — fueling suspicion. Vote ends 5/27. If rejected: strike risk immediately returns + DX/DS rift makes renegotiation far more complex.

escalatedRisks.risk: Goldman Sachs global NAND 4.2% deficit (largest since 2011) 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1. Highest-level independent NAND supply quantification.
escalatedRisks.detail: Global NAND flash deficit of 4.2% forecast for 2026 — largest since 2011. NAND Q2 contract +70-75% QoQ (largest quarterly increase in a decade). Enterprise SSD 53.8% of market — enterprise procurement directly competing with cloud/AI for limited NAND capacity. Combined with full-chain sold-out, Kioxia 2026 sold out, Samsung warm-down production cut, and MLC NAND tripled — the supply-demand imbalance has reached levels justifying structural crisis terminology.

Micron 9550 Pro 5-reference cluster ($16,939-$19,573) remains solid.

escalatedRisks.risk: Former Samsung exec predicts DDR5 price fall H2 2027 from China expansion 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1. First credible timeline for price relief.
escalatedRisks.source: Kye Kyung-hyun speech at National Academy of Engineering Seoul May 2026
escalatedRisks.detail: Former Samsung semiconductor chief Kye Kyung-hyun predicts DDR5 memory prices could fall significantly H2 2027-early 2028 due to aggressive DRAM capacity expansion by Chinese firms (CXMT, Jiahe Jinwei). Forecast: global memory production capacity reaching 6 million wafers/month by H2 2027. Also warns that AI infrastructure spending slowdown after 2028 (if returns fail to justify investment) could trigger severe oversupply. First credible timeline for price normalization — but at 14-18 months out. DDR5 currently +414% vs July 2025 in Germany.
escalatedRisks.confidence: medium (single-source forecast, 14-18 month horizon introduces significant uncertainty)
